prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Arr, gather ‘round, ye swabs, and lend an ear to an old salt’s reckonin’! 

The sky be blue on account o’ the Almighty’s own sea poured up above us. Ye see, the heavens be a vast ocean of air, and when the sun’s golden light comes a-sailin’ through, it scatters like shot from a cannon. The blue light, bein’ a spry and tricksome scallywag, gets tossed about more’n the rest—so ‘tis the colour what fills our eyes when we gaze aloft. 

The sun itself be white as a bone, aye, but the air steals the reds and yellows ‘til only that sailor’s hue remains. So next time ye look to the horizon, remember: ‘tis not just empty ether, but the Lord’s own azure sea, reflectin’ back at us from the very air we breathe. 

Now, fetch me a tot o’ rum afore I ponder deeper on the stars!
